title: Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA)
text:
Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) - SupportGoWhere
Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA)
Share link
A legal document that allows you (the 'Donor') to appoint one or more trusted persons (the 'Donee') to make decisions on your behalf in the event you lose mental capacity.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Fees payable
Referral not required
Collapse all sections
About this support

An LPA allows you to appoint trusted individuals to make decisions on (i) personal welfare and (ii) property and affairs, on your behalf in the event you lose mental capacity.




Who is this for?

Donors must:

Be 21 years old and above

Have the mental capacity to make your own decisions

Not be an undischarged bankrupt if you wish to make an LPA for property and affairs matters

Donees must:

Be 21 years old and above

Be your spouse or partner, family member or relative, friend, or a licensed trust company for property and affair matters only

Have the mental capacity to make their own decisions

What to expect?

Here are the steps on how to make an LPA:

Step 1: Decide on the type of LPA form

LPA Form 1 grants general powers to Donee(s) who will make decisions on your behalf when you lose mental capacity

LPA Form 2 is for applicants who want to appoint more than 2 Donees and/or more than 1 replacement Donee, and grant specific or customised powers that are not in the scope of LPA Form 1. You will need to engage a lawyer for LPA Form 2.

Step 2: Find an LPA certificate issuer who is:

A medical professional accredited by the OPG

A registered psychiatrist

A practising lawyer (excluding lawyers registered under section 36E of the Legal Profession Act 1966)

You and the LPA certificate issuer must digitally sign the LPA using the Singpass app. Your Donees can sign electronically by accepting their appointment. 

For your LPA to be legally valid, the certificate issuer must submit the LPA application to Office of the Public Guardian (OPG) via OPG Online (OPGO), as authorised by you.




How to apply?

You can:

Apply online or

You may also book an appointment or visit a ServiceSG Centre today for in-person guidance and assistance 

for your application. Please note that a LPA certificate issuer is required to ensure that the donor has the mental capacity to make a LPA.




Information last updated on 26 Sep 2025
